response.global_summary: The video showcases a virtual performer, Rindo Chihaya, holding a birthday celebration event. It begins with her singing a cover of the song 'Wataridori' while playing the guitar. Following this, the scene shifts to an instrumental performance where she plays 'Shiny Smily Story (2022 ver.)' on the trombone, without any accompanying vocals.

scenes.description: Rindo Chihaya is performing 'Wataridori' live on stage, playing an acoustic guitar and singing. She is wearing a black and white outfit with green accents. The stage has large screens displaying her image, and there are many glowing penlights in the audience.

I wanna fly so high
Yeah, I know my wings are dried

Well, see you one day

僕等一心に羽ばたいて

scenes.reference_lyrics_en:
I wanna fly so high
Yeah, I know my wings are dried
"Raise your wings," people say
But it's only heartless beyond
Ones who fly, ones who fall
People pay no attention
They don't care at all
But I kept on flying
Just wanted to put the hurt
And bring them away
Chase. We'll make it there
Forcing the wings to flutter
All the nights spent questioning
They look even brighter
Now leave them like a sea bird
Stories you can't even imagine
Watch me, I'll live in em
I wanna fly so far
Away with my guitar
"You're not alone," people sing
It's not wrong
It's not a fantasy
I just don't want to lean on it
No one's listening
They don't care at all
But I kept on singing
So I can turn your hurt
To a smile
I'll get there with this wind
Forcing the wings to flutter
If you're caught in past
I'll take you to awaiting moments
All together like birds migrating
Till the day we complete this musical bigger than us
All this time we come and we grow
Now it's time that we should go
But we both know that this is for sure
It's not the end of the world
Well, see you one day
Chase. We'll make it there
Forcing the wings to flutter
All the nights spent questioning
Tints into the orange of sunrise
I'll happen to appear again someday like a sea bird
Stories you can't even imagine
Until the day we meet again

scenes.description: The scene transitions to Rindo Chihaya performing an instrumental trombone cover of 'Shiny Smily Story (2022 ver.)' on a stage decorated for her birthday event, with large video screens behind her showing her playing. The audience continues to wave their penlights.

response.thoughts:
**Analyzing the VTuber Performance**

Okay, so I've got this video of Rindo Chihaya, apparently celebrating #輪堂千速生誕祭2026. The task is to identify the songs and lyrics. Here's my breakdown:

First, I need to analyze the video content itself. From 0:00 to 0:50, she's on guitar, and I can hear vocals. The on-screen text says "ワタリドリ" (Wataridori). Since I'm tasked with detecting *all* vocal songs, this is definitely a "yes". The original artist is [Alexandros]. So far, so good.

Now, things get interesting from 0:50 to 5:01. She switches to the trombone, and "Shiny Smily Story (2022 ver.)" appears on screen. The crucial question is whether this instrumental performance counts as a "vocal song playing." The instructions specify "active music with sung vocals," and there are *no* sung vocals in *this* part of the video. It's an instrumental cover. I need to make a judgment call here, even though the *original* "Shiny Smily Story" is a vocal track. Since I'm tasked with detecting *all vocal songs playing*, I have to be precise: for this particular scene, `contains_vocal_music` will be `false`.

Next, the metadata. Song 1 is "ワタリドリ" (Wataridori) by [Alexandros]. I'll need to get the lyrics for that. Song 2 is "Shiny Smily Story (2022 ver.)" by Hololive Idol Project. However, I won't need lyrics for this segment because this specific performance doesn't have sung vocals. I must remember that.

My search strategy will be straightforward: I'll use `default_api:web_search_langsearch_multi`. I'll run two queries: "ワタリドリ [Alexandros] 歌詞" for the original Japanese lyrics, and "Wataridori Alexandros lyrics English" for an English translation, for the *first* scene.

Finally, the JSON response. My `global_summary` will be: The video showcases Rindo Chihaya performing a vocal cover of "Wataridori" by [Alexandros], immediately followed by an instrumental trombone performance of "Shiny Smily Story (2022 ver.)" by the Hololive Idol Project.

For the `scenes` part:

*   Scene 1: 00:00.000 to 00:50.000, `contains_vocal_music`: True, Song: "ワタリドリ", Artist: [Alexandros].
*   Scene 2: 00:50.000 to 05:01.000, `contains_vocal_music`: False, Song: "Shiny Smily Story (2022 ver.)", Artist: Hololive Idol Project.

I'm ready to run the searches. I think this will be correct.



Here's my take on the situation:

**Wataridori Lyrics and Instrumental Confirmation - Ready to Proceed**

Alright, perfect. I've successfully located both the Japanese and English lyrics for "Wataridori" - that's a key piece of the puzzle. Beyond that, I can confirm with absolute certainty that the second part of the piece is indeed an instrumental section. This is fantastic; the instrumental section will require a different kind of analysis. With this core information locked down, I'm confident I have everything I need to move forward with the analysis. I'm ready to dig in and understand the nuances of this piece now that I have a comprehensive overview.
